wikitext:
{{Card Infobox|Hang||2}} {{Power Infobox|Hang|2}}
{{C|Hang||2}} is a 1 {{Icon|NE|2}} cost {{QueryLink|Cards|rarity:Rare&color:Necrobinder|Rare|2}} {{QueryLink|Cards|type:Attack&color:Necrobinder|Attack|2}} Card for the {{KW|Necrobinder||2}}. It deals 10 damage (13 if {{KW|Upgrade|Upgraded|2}}). Additionally, if the enemy has no {{BD|Hang||2}}, it applies 2 stacks of {{BD|Hang||2}}, causing ALL {{C|Hang|Hangs|2}} to deal double damage to that enemy. If the enemy already has {{BD|Hang||2}}, it instead doubles the existing {{BD|Hang||2}} stacks.

== Interactions ==
=== Order ===
* {{C|Hang||2}} deals damage before {{BD|Hang||2}} is applied.
* The damage multiplier of {{BD|Hang||2}} applies after {{KW|Strength||2}}, effectively multiplying the player's existing {{KW|Strength||2}} when using {{C|Hang||2}} to attack.

=== Synergies ===
==== a) Cards ====
* Since {{C|Hang||2}} is a Rare card, owning multiple copies may be difficult. Instead, cycle through the deck quickly with draw power like {{C|Soul|Souls|2}} or {{C|Parse||2}}.
* {{C|Misery||2}} applies the {{BD|Hang||2}} stacks on an enemy to ALL other enemies.
* Adding {{KW|Replay||2}} to {{C|Hang||2}}, such as with {{C|Transfigure||2}} or {{C|Hidden Gem||2}}, makes its damage scale much faster.

==== b) Relics ====
* {{R|Unsettling Lamp||2}} causes {{C|Hang||2}} to apply 4 {{BD|Hang||2}} instead of 2 if it is the first debuff-applying card played this combat.
* {{R|Pael's Growth||2}} makes it easy to duplicate {{C|Hang|Hangs|2}}, though it prevents them from getting offensive {{KW|Enchant|Enchantments|2}} like {{KW|Corrupted||2}}.
* {{R|Music Box||2}} creates an {{KW|Ethereal||2}} {{C|Hang||2}} in hand if {{C|Hang||2}} is the first Attack played that turn.

== Notes ==
* {{BD|Artifact||2}} prevents enemies from being afflicted with {{BD|Hang||2}}. When relying on {{C|Hang||2}} to deal major damage, make sure to have debuff-inflicting cards handy to remove {{BD|Artifact||2}} stacks.

== Update History ==
* '''Early Access v0.98'''
** Added Hang to the game.
